message:
viz: add 4 E2E test suites (heat stripe, outlier, phase map, mini-map)

- bd-24q.13.4: Heat Stripe E2E (7 scenarios: tooltip, click-to-jump, bin selection)
- bd-24q.10.4: Outlier E2E (8 scenarios: panel render, metric/topK change, click-to-jump, chart markers)
- bd-24q.11.4: Phase Map E2E (7 scenarios: overlay render, segment coverage, filter opacity, hazard slider)
- bd-24q.2.6: Mini-Map E2E (8 scenarios: toggle, heading jump, markers, search, mobile sheet)

Closed parent beads: bd-24q.10, bd-24q.13, bd-24q.11, bd-24q.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-24q.11","title":"Viz: Phase Map Overlay (Change Points + Regime Segments)","description":"Goal
- Turn the evolution into readable "phases": detect change points in the metric series (lev/tokens/lines and/or bucket-weighted), then overlay regime segments directly on the charts and timeline dock.

UX (Desktop)
- Toggle "Phase overlay": charts show softly tinted phase bands + labels (Phase 1..N). Hover shows segment stats (duration, mean, variance, top buckets).
- Clicking a phase band filters commit list + section summary to that segment.

UX (Mobile)
- Phase bands visible but subtle; phase details appear in a bottom sheet on tap.

Implementation Notes (Alien-Artifact Leaning)
- Use principled change-point detection (BOCPD with explicit hazard + likelihood model) and expose an evidence ledger per detected change (posterior mass / score).
- Prefer explainable segmentation over ad-hoc thresholds; allow selecting the driving metric.
- Cache computed phases in worker/localStorage by dataset hash.

Acceptance Criteria
- Phases feel stable and useful (not flickery); user can understand what changed and when.

Testing
- Unit tests using synthetic piecewise-stationary sequences; assert detected change points within tolerance.
- E2E tests: toggle phase overlay, tap a phase, verify filtering/jump behavior.","notes":"Definition of done (plan-level gate):
- All child implementation subtasks are complete.
- Child unit + e2e beads are implemented and passing with structured logs/artifacts.
- UX validated on desktop and mobile with no critical regressions in navigation or readability.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:47:16.914843010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:17:09.018808439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["spec","viz"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24q.11","depends_on_id":"bd-24q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:47:16.914843010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24q.11","depends_on_id":"bd-24q.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:58:31.879476771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24q.11","depends_on_id":"bd-24q.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:58:31.791878306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-24q.12","title":"Viz: Commit-Time vs Wall-Clock Binning Toggle","description":"Goal
- Let users view the same evolution in two time coordinate systems:
  - Commit-time (index order): emphasizes sequence of edits.
  - Wall-clock time bins (day/hour/15m/5m): emphasizes bursts and pauses.

UX
- A simple toggle (Commit-time | Wall-clock) near the resolution controls.
- When wall-clock is on, bins that have 0 commits should still render (to show quiet periods).

Implementation Notes
- Requires reliable timestamp parsing for commits; define a single canonical timezone for binning (likely local or UTC, but must be explicit and consistent).
- Aggregation functions must be well-defined for all metrics (sum for counts, mean/median for rates); document each.
- Persist in URL state.

Acceptance Criteria
- Switching modes is instantaneous after first compute; charts and heat stripe update consistently.

Testing
- Unit tests for bin boundaries across DST and timezone offsets (use fixed ISO timestamps).
- E2E: toggle mode and verify bin count / labels change deterministically.","notes":"Definition of done (plan-level gate):
- All child implementation subtasks are complete.
- Child unit + e2e beads are implemented and passing with structured logs/artifacts.
- UX validated on desktop and mobile with no critical regressions in navigation or readability.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:47:50.352051887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T02:46:47.229481524Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T02:46:47.229460204Z","close_reason":"All children complete: bd-24q.12.1 (wall-clock series builder), bd-24q.12.2 (UI + URL state), bd-24q.12.3 (unit tests ~50 assertions), bd-24q.12.4 (E2E tests 7 scenarios). Full binning toggle with commit/day/hour/15m/5m resolutions, UTC/local timezone modes, groups/lines/tokens/lev metrics, empty bin filling, URL persistence, and comprehensive test coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["spec","viz"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24q.12","depends_on_id":"bd-24q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:47:50.352051887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24q.12","depends_on_id":"bd-24q.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:58:31.097927643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-24q.14.1","title":"Clustering: Compute MinHash Signatures (Worker)","description":"Implement MinHash signature generation:
- Token shingling on diff text (configurable: full diff vs added-lines-only; shingle size k).
- Deterministic hash functions seeded from dataset hash so results are stable.

 {"id":"bd-24q.15","title":"Viz: Side-by-Side Markdown Panes (A vs B) + Synced Scroll","description":"Goal
- In A/B compare mode, add a side-by-side rendered markdown view (A on left, B on right) with synced scrolling and clear change cues. This is the fastest way to grok "what changed" while keeping the spec readable.

UX (Desktop)
- Two panes with a draggable divider; optional "sync scroll" toggle (on by default).
- Sync by headings/anchors when possible; fallback to proportional scroll.
- Hovering a heading path highlights the corresponding section in both panes.

UX (Mobile)
- Stacked/segmented control: A | B | Split (if landscape).
- Sync via "jump to same heading" rather than continuous scroll.

Implementation Notes
- Reuse reconstructed snapshots from A/B compare; avoid double recompute.
- Provide a robust mapping between A and B headings (by normalized heading text + path).

Acceptance Criteria
- Side-by-side mode is usable on large docs without jank.
- Heading jumps land at consistent corresponding sections.

 {"id":"bd-24q.16","title":"Viz: Inline Changed-Line Highlights in Rendered Markdown","description":"Goal
- Let users read the spec as a document *and still see what changed* inline: highlight added/changed lines/blocks directly in the rendered markdown pane (not only in Diff2Html).

- Same toggle in sheet; changed blocks are navigable via "Next change" button (big tap target).

- Inline highlights are accurate enough to be trusted; toggling is instant.
